title: Magnetic Quantum Criticality inside the Superconducting State Revealed by Penetration Depth Scaling with Local $T_{\mathrm c}$
authors: Yusuke Iguchi, Kaede Inoh, Ryosuke Koizumi, Makoto Yokoyama
abstract: We demonstrate a magnetic quantum critical point embedded within the superconducting state of Zn-doped CeCoIn$_5$, revealed by a pronounced peak in the magnetic penetration depth at zero temperature $λ(0)$. Using scanning SQUID microscopy, we determine the local superconducting transition temperature $T_{\mathrm c}$ and $λ(0)$. By parameterizing $λ(0)$ in terms of the local $T_{\mathrm c}$ rather than nominal Zn substitution, we circumvent the ambiguity caused by doping inhomogeneity and enable a more precise extraction of the critical exponent. The extracted exponent exceeds the clean spin-density-wave value, indicating a disorder-modified quantum critical regime. The enhancement of $λ(0)$ reflects the suppression of the superfluid stiffness and is consistent with critical scaling. Our approach provides a route to uncover intrinsic quantum critical behavior hidden by inhomogeneity in unconventional superconductors.
summary: This study uses scanning SQUID microscopy to reveal a magnetic quantum critical point hidden within the superconducting state of Zn-doped CeCoIn5. By using local transition temperature as a tuning parameter, the authors demonstrate that the quantum critical behavior is modified by disorder, evidenced by a peak in the magnetic penetration depth and an increased critical exponent.
main_problem: Determining the microscopic origin of the antiferromagnetic quantum critical point (AF-QCP) embedded within the superconducting state of Zn-doped CeCoIn5 and distinguishing intrinsic critical behavior from disorder-induced broadening.
main_result: The researchers identified a pronounced peak in the magnetic penetration depth at zero temperature near the AF-QCP and found that the extracted critical exponent exceeds the clean spin-density-wave value, suggesting a disorder-modified quantum critical regime.
method: Scanning SQUID microscopy was used to perform local magnetometry and susceptometry, allowing for the extraction of local Tc and penetration depth by parameterizing data with local Tc rather than nominal doping.
model_or_system: The physical system is the heavy-fermion superconductor CeCo(In1-xZnx)5 in the low-doping regime, where Zn substitution induces antiferromagnetic order and competes with superconductivity.
key_observables: Magnetic penetration depth lambda(0), local superconducting transition temperature Tc, Néel temperature TN, and magnetic susceptibility chi.
important_parameters: Critical exponent nu, impurity scattering strength Gamma, and the temperature dependence of paramagnetic susceptibility alpha.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes that using local Tc as a tuning parameter can circumvent the ambiguity of doping inhomogeneity, and certain data points were excluded due to extreme microscopic inhomogeneity.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows phase diagrams of Tc versus effective impurity scattering, the relationship between Tc and TN, and a comparison of penetration depth data against quantum critical scaling models.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of magnetism in doped CeCoIn5, describes the scanning SQUID experimental setup, presents the local Tc and penetration depth measurements, performs scaling analysis to extract critical exponents, and concludes with the implications for disorder-modified quantum criticality.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Antiferromagnetic quantum criticality in the dirty d-wave superconductor CeCo(In1−xZnx)5 . (a) Nominal x is rescaled to Γ/πTc0, where Γ is the effective impurity scat- tering strength and Tc0=2.27 K for pristine CeCoIn5. Tc fol- lows Abrikosov–Gor’kov (AG) theory [22] in the supercon- ducting (SC) region and deviates in the coexisting SC and AF region. Tc data are taken from Refs. [8, 15]. The top axis indicates the nominal x inferred from the polynomial fit (dashed line), and is shown as a visual guide for panels (b)–(e). (b) Phase diagram as a function of Tc, showing Tc and N´eel temperature TN from Refs. [8, 15]. (c) λ(0) com- pared with a quantum critical scaling model [6] and a...

FIG. 2. Scanning SQUID magnetometry over an isolated vortex visualizes the enhancement of the penetration depth λ near AF-QCP of CeCo(In1−xZnx)5 . (a) Magnetic flux scan over an isolated vortex around 60 mK. (b) The horizontal cross-section of (a) fits the monopole model with the fitting parameter λ. The smallest peak height at x = 0.045a indicates the enhancement of λ near AF-QCP.

FIG. 3. Scanning SQUID Susceptometry at low tem- peratures in CeCo(In1−xZnx)5 reveals an enhancement of λ(0) toward AF-QCP and an increase in the low-temperature exponent n with Zn doping. (a) The temperature de- pendence of M(z = z0) at several locations of x = 0B, 0.03B, 0.045aD, 0.06B (see Fig. S6 for additional loca- tions; local Tc values for each location are listed in supple- mental Table I). The SQUID sensor just touches the sample surface at z0. (b) Superfluid density λ(0)2/λ(T)2 at low tem- peratures. The data are analyzed using the power-law form λ(T) −λ(0) = (T/Tc)n.
